550_spyder.name: Porsche 550 Spyder
550_spyder.notes: The car that established Porsche as a racing marque. James Dean's 'Little Bastard' was a 550.

718_rsk.name: Porsche 718 RSK
718_rsk.notes: Evolution of 550. Won on pure handling vs more powerful rivals.

904_gts.name: Porsche 904 Carrera GTS
904_gts.notes: First Porsche with fiberglass body. Also road-legal.

906.name: Porsche 906 Carrera 6
906.notes: Last customer racing car before FIA homologation rules changed.

917.name: Porsche 917
917.notes: The most iconic Porsche race car ever. 917/30 produced 1,100hp in Can-Am trim — most powerful racing car of its era. Steve McQueen film 'Le Mans' featured the 917.

935.name: Porsche 935
935.notes: Based on 911 Turbo (930). 'Baby' 935 raced in 2.0L class. Moby Dick variant had 845hp.

936.name: Porsche 936
936.notes: Open-top Group 6 prototype. Jacky Ickx drove 1981 Le Mans winner solo for final stint.

956.name: Porsche 956
956.notes: First race car to use ground effect aerodynamics in endurance racing. Set 6:11.13 Nurburgring record in 1983 that stood for 35 years. 1-2-3 finish at Le Mans 1982.

962.name: Porsche 962
962.notes: American version of 956 (modified for IMSA rules). Dominated IMSA for a decade. Dauer 962 Le Mans won 1994 Le Mans as a GT car.

gt1_98.name: Porsche 911 GT1
gt1_98.notes: Mid-engine race car wearing 911 bodywork. 1998 Le Mans win was Porsche's 16th overall. Road car version: 25 built.

rs_spyder.name: Porsche RS Spyder (9R6)
major_wins: Overall ALMS wins vs LMP1 cars
rs_spyder.notes: LMP2 car that regularly beat LMP1 machinery. Penske Racing partnership.

911_rsr.name: Porsche 911 RSR (991)
major_wins: WEC GTE Manufacturers Championship multiple years
911_rsr.notes: Mid-engine 911 (engine moved ahead of rear axle). Most successful modern GT race car.

919_hybrid.name: Porsche 919 Hybrid
919_hybrid.notes: Triple Le Mans winner. 919 Evo (unrestricted) set all-time Nurburgring lap record of 5:19.55 in 2018, breaking the 35-year-old 956 record.

963_lmdh.name: Porsche 963
963_lmdh.notes: LMDh prototype built with Multimatic. Porsche's return to prototype racing after 919 program ended.
